name: Castillo de Santa Catalina por el sendero la Conejera  - circular desde Jaén
region_intro: Mountain biking around Jaén offers diverse terrain across its natural parks and extensive trail networks. The region is characterized by significant mountain ranges like Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas, and Sierra Mágina, featuring karstic landscapes, dense forests, and river valleys. Riders can explore everything from challenging mountain trails with significant elevation gains to more accessible routes through vast olive groves and along converted railway lines. Best mountain bike trails in Jaén The most popular mountain bike trail is Archaeological Route of the Towers, a 26.0 miles (41.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 50 minutes to complete. This moderate route connects historical sites through varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Castillo de Santa Catalina por el sendero la Conejera - circular desde Jaén, a moderate 20.3 miles (32.7 km) path. This circular route offers views of Jaén city and its historic castle. Local mountain bikers also love the Jabalcuz and El Pincho Loop, a 18.5 miles (29.9 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain with notable climbs, often completed in about 2 hours 58 minutes. Mountain biking around Jaén is defined by its diverse mountain ranges, extensive olive groves, and dramatic river gorges. The network offers options for various ability levels, from challenging climbs in natural parks to gentler paths. The routes in Jaén are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 3000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Jaén's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Jaén?
A: Jaén offers a vast network of mountain bike trails, with over 120 routes documented on komoot. These range from easy paths through olive groves to challenging climbs within its natural parks.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Jaén?
A: The best seasons for mountain biking in Jaén are generally spring and autumn. During these times, the weather is mild and pleasant, avoiding the intense summer heat and potential winter rains. Summer can be very hot, especially in lower elevations, while winter can bring cooler temperatures and occasional precipitation, particularly in the higher mountain ranges.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly mountain bike trails in Jaén?
A: Yes, Jaén offers several easier options suitable for families or beginners. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993840343/castillo-de-fuentetejar-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Fuentetéjar Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy route, and the Via Verde del Aceite (Oil Greenway) provides a flatter, accessible experience through olive groves, perfect for a relaxed ride. Many forest tracks in the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park also feature gentle slopes.

Q: Where can I find challenging mountain bike trails in Jaén?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Jaén's natural parks are ideal. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990704373/jabalcuz-and-el-pincho-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jabalcuz and El Pincho Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route with significant climbs. The Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park and Sierra Mágina Natural Park offer demanding terrains and substantial elevation gains, such as the Gilillo Peak Circular Route from Cazorla.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Jaén?
A: Yes, many routes in Jaén are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990715861/castillo-de-santa-catalina-por-el-sendero-la-conejera-circular-desde-jaen" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Santa Catalina por el sendero la Conejera - circular desde Jaén&lt;/a&gt;, offering views of the city and its historic castle. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993840343/castillo-de-fuentetejar-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Fuentetéjar Loop&lt;/a&gt; is another circular option, suitable for an easier ride.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see while mountain biking in Jaén?
A: Jaén's trails often pass by stunning natural and historical sites. In Despeñaperros Natural Park, you can see dramatic geological formations like Los Órganos. The Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park features karstic landscapes, lakes, streams, and waterfalls, including the birth of the Guadalquivir River. You can also explore mountain peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2876708" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro del Tambor Summit&lt;/a&gt; or natural monuments such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4754151" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrance to the Sierra Mágina Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is parking available at mountain bike trailheads in Jaén?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. In natural parks like Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas, and Despeñaperros, designated parking areas are often provided near popular access points. For routes closer to urban centers, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993186324/archaeological-route-of-the-towers" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Archaeological Route of the Towers&lt;/a&gt;, you may find parking in nearby towns or villages.

Q: Are permits required for mountain biking in Jaén's natural parks?
A: While general access to most trails in Jaén's natural parks is usually free, some specific areas or activities might require permits, especially for protected zones or large organized events. It's always advisable to check the official regulations of the specific natural park (e.g., Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas, Sierra Mágina, Despeñaperros) you plan to visit before your ride.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Jaén?
A: The mountain bike trails in Jaén are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars. Riders frequently praise the diverse terrain, from challenging mountain ranges to scenic olive groves, and the well-maintained paths that offer options for all skill levels. The stunning natural landscapes and historical points of interest along the routes are also commonly highlighted.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls or rivers?
A: Yes, the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park is particularly known for its water features. The Borosa River Trail within this park showcases stunning scenery with streams and waterfalls, including the source of the Guadalquivir River. Despeñaperros Natural Park also features the dramatic Despeñaperros river gorge.

Q: Can I find accommodation or places to eat near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, Jaén province offers various accommodation options and eateries, especially in towns and villages bordering the natural parks or along popular routes. For instance, near the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas, you'll find numerous services catering to outdoor enthusiasts. Even routes closer to Jaén city, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990715861/castillo-de-santa-catalina-por-el-sendero-la-conejera-circular-desde-jaen" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Santa Catalina circular route&lt;/a&gt;, are conveniently located near urban amenities.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails with significant viewpoints?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Jaén, especially those in the mountainous natural parks, offer breathtaking viewpoints. Routes ascending to higher elevations in Sierra Mágina or the Sierras de Cazorla provide panoramic vistas of the diverse landscapes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990715861/castillo-de-santa-catalina-por-el-sendero-la-conejera-circular-desde-jaén" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Santa Catalina circular route&lt;/a&gt; also offers excellent views over Jaén city and its surroundings.
tips: It has a couple of slightly narrow 180º turns and is shared with people doing sports, so you have to be careful.

Path or trail for MTB descent whose popular name is "La Conejera". It has a total length of 1.5 kilometers until its exit to the track and its difficulty according to the German STS scale is S2 or medium difficulty.

On the summit of Cerro de Santa Catalina it was already raised in the s. IX an old citadel of caliphal origin. A century later, after the conquest of the city by Fernando III in 1246, the Christian castle was built, which has survived to this day.
